name: Pope Luna and Mesones de Isuela Castles Loop
name_original: Nigüella - Mesones de Isuela - Castillo del Papa Luna — circular desde Illueca
description: This difficult 17.2-mile touring cycling route from Illueca explores historical castles and agricultural landscapes in Aragon.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Illueca offers routes through the diverse landscapes of the Comarca of Aranda in Aragon, Spain. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including parts of the Moncayo Natural Park and the Sierra de la Virgen. Cyclists can navigate varied elevations, with features like Pico Cabrera and Alto de la Dehesa contributing to the challenging topography. This area provides a blend of natural features and historical sites suitable for touring cycling exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Illueca The most popular touring cycling route is Pope Luna and Mesones de Isuela Castles Loop, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete, passing by significant historical castles. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Calcena – Calcena loop from Illueca, a difficult 39.8 miles (64.1 km) path offering panoramic views of the Calcena area. Local touring cyclists also love the Calatayud – ️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Illueca, a 45.5 miles (73.2 km) trail leading through the varied terrain surrounding Calatayud, often completed in about 4 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Illueca is defined by mountainous terrain, the Moncayo Natural Park, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, including challenging routes with significant ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Illueca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Illueca?
A: Touring cycling around Illueca primarily features mountainous terrain, including parts of the Moncayo Natural Park and the Sierra de la Virgen. You can expect varied elevations, with significant ascents and descents, as indicated by features like Pico Cabrera and Alto de la Dehesa. The region offers a challenging yet rewarding landscape for cyclists.

Q: How challenging are the touring cycling routes around Illueca?
A: The touring cycling routes around Illueca are generally considered difficult. All 17 routes listed in this guide are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and requiring a good level of fitness. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994096538/pope-luna-and-mesones-de-isuela-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pope Luna and Mesones de Isuela Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes over 570 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Illueca?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Illueca are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994096538/pope-luna-and-mesones-de-isuela-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pope Luna and Mesones de Isuela Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calcena – Calcena loop from Illueca&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31887832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Illueca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites can I visit while touring cycling in Illueca?
A: The region is rich in history. You can cycle past significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle-Palace of Pope Luna&lt;/a&gt; in Illueca, the birthplace of Pope Luna. The municipality of Mesones de Isuela also boasts one of Aragon's largest and best-preserved medieval castles, which you can see on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994096538/pope-luna-and-mesones-de-isuela-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pope Luna and Mesones de Isuela Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6280360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierga&lt;/a&gt;, with its Mudejar-style bell tower, is another notable stop.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can find natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2920672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Gollizno&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera (1,433 m)&lt;/a&gt; in the Sierra de la Virgen, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5834920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Mesones de Isuela and Moncayo from the Descent to Nigüella&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Illueca?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Illueca typically aligns with the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally more stable. Summers can be quite hot, especially in July and August, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow in higher elevations of the Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easier touring cycling options for beginners or families?
A: The touring cycling routes specifically listed in this guide are all rated as difficult, featuring significant climbs and distances. However, the broader 'Cara Oculta Bike Park' in the Comarca of Aranda, which encompasses the Illueca area, does offer a range of routes, including more leisurely 'rouleur rally routes' suitable for those new to off-road biking or seeking a relaxed ride. These might be a better starting point for beginners or families.

Q: What are some of the longer touring cycling routes available from Illueca?
A: For longer touring cycling experiences, you might consider routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31887832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Illueca&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 73.2 km (45.5 miles) and typically takes around 4 hours 40 minutes. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calcena – Calcena loop from Illueca&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 64.1 km (39.8 miles).

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: While the guide doesn't specify cafes on every route, the region's towns and villages, such as Illueca, Mesones de Isuela, and Tierga, offer opportunities for rest and refreshments. Planning your route to pass through these settlements will allow you to find local establishments to refuel.

Q: Is it possible to combine touring cycling with exploring other areas nearby?
A: Absolutely. Illueca is situated in Aragon, a region recognized for its diverse cycling tourism. The Moncayo Natural Park is a significant feature, and the 'Cara Oculta Bike Park' offers 25 all-terrain biking routes across the Comarca of Aranda. This provides ample opportunity to explore varied landscapes and trails beyond the immediate vicinity of Illueca.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on these touring cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Illueca feature significant elevation gains due to the mountainous terrain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Armantes loop from Aniñón&lt;/a&gt; involves over 539 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcena – Trasobares loop from Trasobares&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 645 meters. These routes are designed for cyclists who enjoy challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints to look out for on the routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5834920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Mesones de Isuela and Moncayo from the Descent to Nigüella&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a picturesque panorama. Routes traversing the Sierra de la Virgen also offer expansive views, especially as you gain elevation.
tips:
The Nigüella Fountain is one of the most prominent heritage sites in the municipality of Nigüella, located in the province of Zaragoza. It is considered a place of historical and traditional importance within the town.

According to tourist guides about Nigüella, the fountain is mentioned as a point of notable interest, described as a "source of life and tradition for the locals." This indicates that it has been an important space in daily life, possibly as a water source and as a social gathering place.

From this point we have a beautiful picture of the imposing Castle of Mesones de Isuela controlling the town from the top of the rocky hill.
Guided tours inside the castle can be booked at the tourist office of the Aranda region

A charming place with a lot of history is the one that we find perched on top of the rock in the town of Illueca. It is the Castle - Palace of Pope Luna (who was born here) that stands out from afar. They make very interesting guided tours (prior reservation) based on the history of the palace itself and of Papa Luna.
There is restaurant and hotel service

faqs.question: Where does the Nigüella - Mesones de Isuela - Castillo del Papa Luna circular route start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: This circular touring bicycle route originates from Illueca. While specific parking details are not provided, as a starting point for a circular route, you can typically find parking within the town of Illueca itself.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is explicitly categorized as a difficult cycling tour. Cyclists should expect challenging ascents and varied surfaces, requiring good physical condition.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain involves challenging ascents and varied surfaces, typical of touring bicycle routes in a hilly, rural Spanish landscape. You will likely encounter agricultural land with olive, almond, and cherry trees, as well as sheep pastures.

faqs.question: What notable sights can I see along the Nigüella - Mesones de Isuela - Castillo del Papa Luna route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can visit the Nigüella Spring. You'll also get a View of Castillo de Mesones de Isuela and pass by the Castle-Palace of Pope Luna in Illueca, which is a Renaissance building built upon a medieval fortress.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this touring bicycle route, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in rural areas with livestock or near roads, and to ensure they are fit enough for a difficult cycling tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Aragon can experience hot summers. Spring and autumn generally offer more moderate temperatures, making them ideal for a difficult cycling tour like this one. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for cycling this route. Access to the natural areas and public roads is generally free.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 26% of this route overlaps with the official trail known as Etapa 5: Tierga - Morata de Jalón.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and rural setting, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, appropriate cycling gear for varied terrain, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Consider layers of clothing for changing weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly rated as 'difficult' and requires good physical condition due to its challenging ascents and varied terrain. It is not recommended for beginner cyclists or families with young children.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the 'Castillo del Papa Luna' mentioned in the route name?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily centered around the Castle-Palace of Pope Luna in Illueca, which is a Renaissance building on a medieval fortress. This is distinct from the more famous Castillo del Papa Luna in Peñíscola, which is a coastal castle far from this route.

waypoints.name: View of Castillo de Mesones de Isuela
